seoTitle: Mile End vs Mile End South — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mile End and Mile End South. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mile End vs Mile End South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mile End and Mile End South.
heading: Mile End vs Mile End South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mile End, South Australia and Mile End South, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mile End South has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Mile End (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mile End or Mile End South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mile End has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Mile End South scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mile End scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Mile End South scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mile End or Mile End South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mile End South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
